author: Garrulity
text:
Anchor Task.repo at claim, not create; guard the basename fallback

Task.repo is code-home provenance. Stamping it at bl create anchored on where the ball was filed — on a bare hub or a forge-sync bridge, not the code repo at all. The clone that runs bl claim is definitionally the code home, so claim is now where repo gets its authoritative value.

bl claim (worktree and --no-worktree paths) re-anchors repo to the claiming clone's origin URL. Only a fetchable URL is ever auto-written: repo_url::current's basename/path fallback is split out as the new repo_url::origin_url, which returns None when there is no origin. bl create and bl claim both take that None and leave repo null rather than persist a bare basename no sibling clone can fetch.

repo is implicitly frozen after claim — no lifecycle step re-stamps it — but not locked: bl update <id> repo=<url> (and repo= / repo=null to clear) is the explicit fixup path when a task's code home changes.
